examples: Immunotherapy is another new approach that stimulates immune cells to enhance their anticancer activity.
For instance, many of the recent and upcoming anticancer drugs are custom-designed biological molecules.
Currently, many anticancer drugs are difficult to deliver to patients due to their difficulty to be soluble in water.
Some patients, apparently, need only a small trigger to propel a massive anticancer attack.
In the long term, genetic signatures are also throwing up new leads for anticancer drug design.
